source: workflow CLI (~/.local/bin/workflow) — a Rust binary with kernel verbs
description: A different universe's articulation of the same problems. We don't call it — we study its contracts as reference for how these problems look when solved as typed JSON. These contracts are the reference shapes for pi-workflows' native constructs: agent output schemas, step output contracts, project block types, verification result types. Same ideas, different runtime — YAML specs + JSON schemas + Nunjucks templates + pi subprocess dispatch instead of Rust kernel verbs.

contracts.name: project-data
contracts.description: Project as structured data: the shape of 'what this project is.'
contracts.maps_to: project.json, decisions.json — project identity and accumulated decisions

contracts.name: state-data
contracts.description: State separate from project: the shape of 'where it is right now.'

shape.description: How the roadmap has changed over time

contracts.maps_to: state.json — current position, accumulated context, session continuity

contracts.name: verifier-output
contracts.description: Verification as a rich typed result: the shape of 'was intent fulfilled, and where's the proof.'

shape.description: Exists/substantive/wired — three levels of artifact checking
How well outputs bind to inputs

contracts.maps_to: verifier-output schema — truths, criteria_results, artifacts, gaps, human_verification

contracts.name: execution-results
contracts.description: What agents produce: the shape of 'what work was done.'
contracts.maps_to: execution-results schema — tasks, decisions, issues, deviations, requirements_completed

contracts.name: roadmap-data
contracts.description: Phases with goals, dependencies, requirements mappings: the shape of 'how intent decomposes into work.'
contracts.maps_to: phases/*.json — phase specs with intent, criteria, status, specs list
